because.text: Accepted in full. M1 confirmed and reproduced at both budgets: the memory fence guarded on spent (an UPPER bound on size, not a proxy), making eval(Omega, n>=limit) fault instead of returning canonical ATP Exhausted - violating TV-7's forall-n and letting differently-configured nodes disagree on an outcome; fixed by the peer instance (129a828, co-authored with the reviewer), verified here (eval(Omega, 2e6) -> ATP Exhausted, spent 1999305), and the causal prose corrected in s3.4 (bound = preflight estimate, never a live fault trigger). Minors all confirmed and fixed in v0.5.2: m1 stale 0.5.0 headers under v0.5.1 anchors (maintainer's own release miss - headers now 0.5.2 with a fresh anchor section); m2 PH_ONLY_LEAVES dead code -> coordinate() accessor + WV-COORD-* vectors, the s2.1 visibility guarantee is now executable; m3 s3.4 one-sentence definition tying materialized_size to the section's metric (confirms the Kimi refutation from the impl side); N1 s4 80-bit demand dropped, arbiter authoritative; N2 three eval vectors pin stuck normal forms and REF-enabled redexes (suite 49); N3 width note for ports. Process note recorded: fix-then-adjudicate is acceptable only with independent verification before the warrant - which this is.

subject.note: Opus 4.8 (1M) adversarial review of v0.5.1 (consensus safety attacked, none broken; 1 MAJOR + 5 hygiene) -> release v0.5.2 'Honest Fences'
